channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's counterterrorism strategy makes targeting drug cartels the top priority
item.description: President Trump has signed off on a new U.S. counterterrorism strategy that sets eliminating drug cartels in the Western Hemisphere as the administration's highest priority.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has signed off on a new U.S. counterterrorism strategy that sets eliminating drug cartels in the Western Hemisphere as the administration's highest priority.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Antisemitism is a campaign issue in UK regional vote
item.description: After a spate of attacks on Jews in Britain, politicians there are accusing each other of antisemitism ahead of local elections.
item.content:encoded: After a spate of attacks on Jews in Britain, politicians there are accusing each other of antisemitism ahead of local elections.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Greetings from a sea village in Indonesia, where Indigenous fishing gets help from mangroves
item.description: As a water taxi rounded a corner at sunset, the village of Torosiaje came into view, with its colorful stilted houses perched over the Molucca Sea.
item.content:encoded: As a water taxi rounded a corner at sunset, the village of Torosiaje came into view, with its colorful stilted houses perched over the Molucca Sea.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Iran is reviewing a U.S. proposal as Trump threatens renewed bombing if it doesn't agree
item.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman said negotiators are assessing the Trump administration's latest proposal and will relate a response to mediating country Pakistan.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman said negotiators are assessing the Trump administration's latest proposal and will relate a response to mediating country Pakistan.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Democrats gain midterm edge, NPR poll shows. And, U.S. pauses 'Project Freedom'
item.description: Democrats outpace Republicans in voter enthusiasm for the midterm elections, according to NPR's latest poll. And Trump says "Project Freedom" is paused because of progress toward an Iran agreement.
item.content:encoded:
Democrats outpace Republicans in voter enthusiasm for the midterm elections, according to NPR's latest poll. And Trump says "Project Freedom" is paused because of progress toward an Iran agreement.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Skeletons in their clothing: Recovering bodies from the rubble in Gaza
item.description: It was one of the deadliest Israeli strikes of the Gaza war, devastating one extended family. A year and a half later, the survivors find their loved ones' remains.
item.content:encoded:
It was one of the deadliest Israeli strikes of the Gaza war, devastating one extended family. A year and a half later, the survivors find their loved ones' remains.

(Image credit: Anas Baba)
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: U.S. stages largest-ever annual military drills in the Philippines
item.description: U.S. and allies hold their largest drills yet in the Philippines near Taiwan, showcasing new tactics and alliances to deter China—while Beijing stages its own exercises nearby.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and allies hold their largest drills yet in the Philippines near Taiwan, showcasing new tactics and alliances to deter China—while Beijing stages its own exercises nearby.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine before announced ceasefire
item.description: Zelenskyy rebuked Moscow for what he said was its "utter cynicism" in launching the attacks after Russia announced a unilateral ceasefire over two days later this week.
item.content:encoded:
Zelenskyy rebuked Moscow for what he said was its "utter cynicism" in launching the attacks after Russia announced a unilateral ceasefire over two days later this week.

(Image credit: Iryna Rybakova)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Police say Australian women with alleged IS ties face charges on return from Syria
item.description: The Australian government had been alerted Wednesday that four women and nine children had booked flights from Damascus to Australia, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said.
item.content:encoded:
The Australian government had been alerted Wednesday that four women and nine children had booked flights from Damascus to Australia, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said.

(Image credit: Baderkhan Ahmad)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. says it's made Hormuz safe for commercial shipping, but standoff hasn't changed
item.description: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.
item.content:encoded: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman
